description_long: This is a rustic, heavy-duty pitchfork with five long, sharply pointed tines extending from a thick, solid base. The tines are evenly spaced and appear slightly curved, giving the tool a robust and utilitarian appearance. The entire object has a rough, weathered surface, suggesting it is made of aged metal, possibly iron, with visible signs of rust and corrosion. The handle is short and merges directly into the base, lacking any modern grip or ergonomic features. The coloration is predominantly a dark, rusty brown, further emphasizing its aged and well-used condition. This pitchfork would be suitable for heavy outdoor work, such as moving hay, debris, or compost in an urban garden or maintenance setting.
description: A weathered, five-tined metal pitchfork with a rough, rusty brown surface and a short, solid handle. The tines are long, sharp, and slightly curved, giving it a robust, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view showing all five pointed tines evenly spaced and the thick base connecting them.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the length and sharpness of the tines and the short, sturdy handle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the alignment of the tines and the solid, flat back of the base.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the curvature and spacing of the tines.
